title: Matelea chiapensis
intro: Matelea chiapensis, also known as the Chiapas Matelea or Mexican Swallowtail Vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and fever, due to its potential antinociceptive, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Matelea chiapensis include alkaloids, specifically 8-oxo-3,4-dihydro-4-β-carbomethoxy-3-methoxyphenanthrene, as well as other compounds that have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In traditional medicine, Matelea chiapensis is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, as well as being used in topical applications, such as ointments and salves.
